ddls:
CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each programming language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the programming language */,
  "intended_use" TEXT /* Intended use of the programming language */,
  "standardized" TEXT /* Standardization status and organizations involved */,
  "year_created" TEXT /* Year when the language was first created */,
  "creator" TEXT /* Name of the individual or organization that created the language */,
  "current_version" TEXT /* The current version of the language */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the language details were last updated */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to the official documentation of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "paradigms" (
  "paradigm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each programming paradigm */,
  "paradigm_name" TEXT /* Name of the programming paradigm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the programming paradigm */,
  "example_languages" TEXT /* A comma-separated list of example languages that support this paradigm */,
  "popularity_score" INTEGER /* A score indicating the popularity of the paradigm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("paradigm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "language_paradigms" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language */,
  "paradigm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming paradigm */,
  "supported_features" TEXT /* A comma-separated list of specific features supported by the language for the given paradigm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id", "paradigm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standardization effort */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization involved in the standardization */,
  "year" TEXT /* Year of standardization */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the standardization effort */,
  "standard_version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the standard */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the official standard document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "language_standards" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the standardization effort */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the language that the standard applies to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id", "standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, researcher, admin) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered on the platform */,
  "last_active_date" TEXT /* Date when the user last accessed the platform */,
  "contributions_count" INTEGER /* Number of contributions made by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the language details */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the language details were accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or tool used to access the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contributions" (
  "contribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contribution */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language being contributed to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the contribution */,
  "contribution_date" TEXT /* Date when the contribution was made */,
  "contribution_type" TEXT /* Type of contribution (e.g., update, new information) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the contribution */,
  "review_status" TEXT /* Status of the contribution (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the contribution */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the contribution was reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contributions_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contributions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contributions_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id")
)
